# Clinical Trial Figure Specification (SAS ODS Graphics / Jenner)

**Scope.** Statistical figures for clinical study reports, regulatory submissions,
and publications: Kaplan–Meier curves, mean-change/LS-means trajectories, forest
plots, waterfall/swimmer/spaghetti plots, scatter and diagnostic plots.

**Applies to:** all figures generated by code in the `CRO/` folder of this
repository. The companion conformance harness
[`clinical_figure_conformance.py`](./clinical_figure_conformance.py)
provides a machine-checkable implementation of every rule in this document.

**Governing principle.** Grayscale-first. The figure must remain fully
interpretable when printed in black and white and when viewed by a reader with
colour-vision deficiency (CVD). Colour is an *accent*, never the sole carrier of
meaning. Every distinction encoded by colour is also encoded by a second channel
(line pattern, marker symbol, or a direct label).

---

## 1. Canvas, dimensions, resolution

Figures are sized to drop into a CSR page without rescaling. Use a single,
documented design size per figure class; do not let SAS auto-size.

| Figure class | Orientation | Design size (in) | Aspect | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| General (line, scatter, KM no-risk-table) | Landscape | 9.0 × 5.56 | 1.62 : 1 | Fits landscape Letter/A4 inside 1″ margins |
| Compact / two-per-page | Landscape | 6.4 × 4.0 | 1.60 : 1 | |
| Full-page portrait | Portrait | 6.5 × 8.0 | 0.81 : 1 | |
| KM with at-risk table | Landscape | 9.0 × 6.0 | 1.50 : 1 | Extra height for risk rows |
| Forest / swimmer | Landscape | 9.0 × (3.0 + 0.22 × rows) | variable | Height scales with row count |

- **Resolution:** 300 dpi for raster output (`IMAGE_DPI=300`). Prefer **vector**
  (route through `ODS PDF`/`ODS PRINTER`, or `IMAGEFMT=SVG`/`EMF`) wherever the
  receiving system accepts it.
- **Format:** PNG for eCTD-embeddable raster; SVG/PDF/EMF for vector.
- **Background:** white (or transparent); **no** wall fill, gradient, 3-D, or shadow.
- **Aspect ratio** is fixed per class so a figure set looks uniform.

**Jenner implementation:** set design size before each graphic PROC:

```sas
/* General / KM */
ods graphics / width=9in height=5.56in;

/* KM with at-risk table */
ods graphics / width=9in height=6in;

/* Forest / swimmer — height scales; set per-figure */
ods graphics / width=9in height=5.5in;  /* adjust for row count */
```

---

## 2. Frame style

**A full box is drawn around the plot wall** — all four sides present,
equal weight. This is the SAS default and the required house style.

- Do **not** use an open L-frame or suppress any border side.
- Do **not** add a drop-shadow, raised-panel, or wall-fill colour.
- Achieved automatically with SAS `PROC SGPLOT` / `PROC LIFETEST` defaults;
  do not override with `NOBORDER` on the plot statement.

---

## 3. Typography

One sans-serif family throughout (Arial / Helvetica; `Albany AMT` is the
metric-compatible SAS default). All text **black**.

| Element | Size | Style |
|---|---|---|
| Title | 10–11 pt | bold |
| Axis label | 9–10 pt | regular |
| Tick / axis values | 8–9 pt | regular |
| Legend | 8–9 pt | regular |
| Data labels, at-risk counts | 7–8 pt | regular |
| Footnotes | 7–8 pt | regular |

Sponsor SOPs may mandate a specific face (often Arial or Times) — that overrides
the default but the *single-family, all-black* rule stands.

---

## 4. Colour palette (Okabe–Ito / Wong, CVD-safe)

Eight values; slot 1 reserved **black** so single-group and control series stay
monochrome. Distinguishable under deuteranopia, protanopia, and tritanopia.

| Slot | Name | Hex | Typical use |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Black | `#000000` | Control / single series / all axes & text |
| 2 | Blue | `#0072B2` | Treatment arm 2 |
| 3 | Vermillion | `#D55E00` | Treatment arm 3 |
| 4 | Bluish green | `#009E73` | Arm 4 |
| 5 | Reddish purple | `#CC79A7` | Arm 5 |
| 6 | Orange | `#E69F00` | Arm 6 |
| 7 | Sky blue | `#56B4E9` | Arm 7 |
| 8 | Yellow | `#F0E442` | **Dark backgrounds only — excluded from line/marker rotation on white** |

**Source:** Okabe & Ito, *Color Universal Design* (2008); Wong, *Nature Methods*
8:441 (2011).

### Yellow exclusion rule

Slot 8 (`#F0E442`) is near-invisible on a white background (relative luminance
0.93, contrast ratio 1.07 : 1 against white). It is **dropped from the
line/marker colour cycle** for all figures in this folder. Reserve it for fills
on dark or contrasting backgrounds only (e.g. poster annotation boxes, dark-theme
dashboard panels).

The active line/marker colour rotation is therefore slots **1 → 7** in order.

---

## 5. Colour-usage rules

1. **Monochrome unless colour earns its place.** Introduce colour only to
   (a) separate ≥ 2 overlapping groups, (b) flag a clinically meaningful
   threshold or region (e.g. RECIST ±20 %/−30 %, ULN, LLOQ), or (c) highlight
   a single focal series against muted others.
2. **Always pair colour with a second channel.** Group = colour **and** line
   pattern **and** marker symbol (redundant encoding). In SAS this is enforced
   with `ATTRPRIORITY=NONE`.
3. **Cap simultaneous colours at ~5.** Beyond that, switch to small multiples
   (panelled lattice) rather than more hues.
4. **Never use red/green as the only contrast pair.** Use the palette's
   blue/orange/black ordering instead.
5. **Reference/threshold lines** are thin dashed/dotted **black**, not coloured,
   unless the threshold itself is the point of the figure.

---

## 6. Lines, markers, reference lines

- **Primary series:** 1.5–2 px; distinct line pattern per group from the palette.
- **CI bands / secondary:** 1 px, or a light semi-transparent fill.
- **Markers:** filled, 6–8 px, distinct symbol per group
  (circle → square → triangle → diamond → star …).
- **Censoring ticks (KM):** small `+`, same colour as the curve.
- **Reference lines:** 1 px, dashed or dotted, black.

---

## 7. Axes, ticks, gridlines, legend

- **Axes:** black, 1 px. Ticks point outward. Label every axis with units.
- **Gridlines:** OFF by default. Enable only when reading exact values matters,
  and then very light gray (`#E0E0E0`).
- **Frame:** **full box** (all four sides). SAS default — do not suppress.
- **Legend:** inside the plot (top-left/right) when space allows, else below;
  no border, no background fill. Order matches a fixed treatment-arm sequence.
- **At-risk table** (KM): aligned to x-axis ticks, 7–8 pt, below the plot.

---

## 8. SAS implementation

The companion program **`clin_graph_style.sas`** (to be created alongside
submissions) provides the enforceable form of this spec:

- `STYLES.CLINGRAPH` — ODS style with the palette, black axes/text, single
  sans-serif family, faint gridlines, dashed black reference lines.
- `CLIN_ATTRMAP` — a discrete attribute map (`DATTRMAP=`) that **locks each
  treatment arm to a fixed colour + pattern + symbol** across every figure in a
  submission, independent of data sort order.
- `%CLIN_ODS_ON` / `%CLIN_ODS_OFF` — open the destination at 300 dpi (or vector)
  with `ATTRPRIORITY=NONE` so redundant encoding is automatic.

Key switches that make the spec real rather than aspirational:

| Requirement | SAS mechanism |
|---|---|
| Redundant colour + pattern + symbol | `ODS GRAPHICS / ATTRPRIORITY=NONE` |
| Identical arm styling everywhere | `DATTRMAP=clin_attrmap` + `ATTRID=trt` |
| Submission resolution | `IMAGE_DPI=300`, `IMAGEFMT=PNG` (or SVG/EMF) |
| No border / clean canvas | `BORDER=OFF`, `NOBORDER` on the plot |
| Gridlines suppressed | `XAXIS/YAXIS DISPLAY=(NOGRID)` |
| Full-box frame | SAS default — no override needed |
| Yellow excluded from rotation | Omit slot 8 from `DATTRMAP` line/marker entries |

### Minimal SAS attribute map (7 arms, yellow excluded)

```sas
data clin_attrmap;
    length id $8 value $20 linecolor $8 linepattern $12
           markercolor $8 markersymbol $12;
    id = 'trt';
    input value $ linecolor $ linepattern $ markercolor $ markersymbol $;
    datalines;
Placebo   #000000  Solid       #000000  CircleFilled
LowDose   #0072B2  ShortDash   #0072B2  SquareFilled
HighDose  #D55E00  ShortDot    #D55E00  TriangleFilled
Arm4      #009E73  DashDot     #009E73  DiamondFilled
Arm5      #CC79A7  LongDash    #CC79A7  StarFilled
Arm6      #E69F00  ThinDot     #E69F00  HomeFilled
Arm7      #56B4E9  LongDashDot #56B4E9  SquareXFilled
;
run;
```

---

## 9. Validation / accessibility checklist

Before a figure is final:

- [ ] Convert to grayscale — are all series still distinguishable?
      (If not, the pattern/symbol encoding is insufficient.)
- [ ] Run through a CVD simulator (deutan/protan/tritan).
- [ ] No information conveyed by colour alone.
- [ ] Minimum line thickness and marker size met; text ≥ 7 pt.
- [ ] Axes labelled with units; reference lines annotated.
- [ ] Figure renders at the documented design size without rescaling.
- [ ] Treatment-arm encoding matches the standard attribute map.
- [ ] Full-box frame present on all four sides.
- [ ] Yellow (`#F0E442`) not used for any line or marker on a white background.

Run the machine checks:

```bash
python CRO/clinical_figure_conformance.py path/to/figure.svg
```

or under pytest:

```bash
pytest CRO/clinical_figure_conformance.py -q
```

---

## 10. Quick-reference: figure size by `ods graphics` statement

```sas
/* Kaplan-Meier without at-risk table */
ods graphics / width=9in height=5.56in reset=index;
proc lifetest data=... plots=survival(atrisk nocensor);
    ...
run;

/* Kaplan-Meier with at-risk table */
ods graphics / width=9in height=6in reset=index;
proc lifetest data=... plots=survival(atrisk);
    ...
run;

/* Forest plot (subgroup HR, 8 rows example) */
ods graphics / width=9in height=4.76in reset=index;  /* 3.0 + 0.22*8 */
proc sgplot data=forest_data noautolegend;
    ...
run;

/* Waterfall / swimmer */
ods graphics / width=9in height=5.56in reset=index;
proc sgplot data=wf_data noautolegend;
    ...
run;

/* Compact (two per page) */
ods graphics / width=6.4in height=4in reset=index;
```
